items.title: [framework] Add chunky DKG Move modules
items.body: ## Summary Adds Move modules for Chunky DKG on-chain state management. ### `chunky_dkg.move` - `ChunkyDKGSessionMetadata` - public input for DKG (dealer epoch, config, validator sets) - `ChunkyDKGStartEvent` - emitted when a session starts - `ChunkyDKGSessionState` - session input/output with metadata, start time, and transcript - `ChunkyDKGState` resource - tracks last completed and in-progress sessions - Functions: `initialize`, `start`, `finish`, `try_clear_incomplete_session` ### `chunky_dkg_config.move` - `ChunkyDKGConfig` - on-chain configuration with variant and secrecy/reconstruct thresholds - Integrated with `config_buffer.move` for config updates Cursor Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no issues for commit d42b92d

items.title: [consensus] Add ValidatorTransaction::ChunkyDKGResult and consensus hooks
items.body:
## Summary

Adds `ValidatorTransaction::ChunkyDKGResult` variant and consensus hooks to check `OnChainChunkyDKGConfig` when validating validator transactions.

items.title: Tune jemalloc configuration for better perf
items.body:
- Per CPU arenas.
- 2MB huge pages.
- Background threads for async maintenance work.
- Longer decay time for dirty pages.
- Small tweaks to thread cache. (40KB allocations are very hot right now, not sure where it comes from.)

See comments in code for more details.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the global allocator and default `jemalloc` runtime settings for core binaries, which can affect performance, memory usage, and stability across platforms.
> 
> **Overview**
> Introduces a new workspace crate, `aptos-jemalloc`, to centralize jemalloc setup and a tuned default `malloc_conf` (per-CPU arenas, THP, background purging threads, longer decay, and thread-cache tweaks) while still allowing override via `MALLOC_CONF`.
> 
> Updates `aptos-node`, `aptos-debugger`, and `aptos-executor-benchmark` to depend on `aptos-jemalloc` on Unix and replace per-binary allocator/malloc_conf boilerplate with `aptos_jemalloc::setup_jemalloc!()`. Also refreshes `testsuite/single_node_performance_values.tsv` baseline numbers to reflect the new allocator configuration.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0b2886e7b1c2b7b9d9194ecf211ee95be2d8aa61. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [docker] Migrate from Debian Bullseye to Trixie
items.body:
## Summary

Migrate Docker base images from Debian Bullseye to Trixie with several improvements:

### Base Image Updates
- `debian:bullseye` → `debian:trixie`
- `rust:1.90.0-bullseye` → `rust:1.91.0-trixie` (full image, not slim)

### Package Changes
- `libssl1.1` → `libssl3` (OpenSSL 3.x)
- `libpq-dev` → `libpq5` (runtime only needs shared library)
- `netcat` → `netcat-openbsd` (package rename)
- Removed `software-properties-common` (Ubuntu-only, not needed)

### APT Configuration
- Use default Debian mirrors (removed cloudfront for simplicity)
- Fixed deprecated `apt-key` → `gpg --dearmor` in tools.Dockerfile
- Removed Bullseye-specific version pins from tools.Dockerfile

### LLVM SHA1 Workaround
- Debian Trixie disabled SHA1 signatures by default (Feb 2025)
- Added sequoia crypto policy to allow SHA1 for LLVM apt repo
- See https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/179148

### Build Cache Updates
- Changed target cache IDs to `*-trixie` to invalidate old Bullseye caches
- Ensures clean rebuild with OpenSSL 3.x headers

### Tool Upgrades
- kubectl: v1.18.6 → v1.35.0
- helm: v3.8.0 → v3.20.0
- Updated kubectl download URL to `dl.k8s.io` (new official location)

### Architecture Support
- Made kubectl/helm downloads architecture-aware (`dpkg --print-architecture`)

### Dockerfile Optimizations (forge.Dockerfile)
- Combined kubectl/helm downloads into single layer
- Reordered layers for better caching (apt → downloads → copies → build-info)
- Added cleanup for helm tarball extraction

## Test plan
- [ ] Build Docker images locally with `docker/docker-bake-rust-all.sh`
- [ ] Verify validator image starts correctly
- [ ] Verify tools image works with aptos CLI
- [ ] Run forge tests with new images

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

items.title: Reduce number of threads for various services
items.body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes runtime sizing for multiple always-on services, which can impact performance and task scheduling under load. Functional logic is unchanged, but misconfiguration or lower defaults could surface latency/regression issues.
> 
> **Overview**
> Makes tokio runtime worker thread counts configurable for several auxiliary services by adding `num_threads: Option<usize>` to `PeerMonitoringServiceConfig`, `AdminServiceConfig`, and `InspectionServiceConfig`, and introducing a new `TelemetryServiceConfig` attached to `NodeConfig`.
> 
> Updates peer monitoring, telemetry, inspection, and admin service startup to pass the configured thread count into `aptos_runtimes::spawn_named_runtime`, changing defaults to smaller fixed values (e.g., peer monitoring defaults to 4; inspection/admin default to 2).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 26c2dd11629697266ebf227dc99349c7fb2442e4. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [Storage] Fix for panics in state update code and improve error messages
items.body:
## Summary
- Cherry-pick of be2fc5a62b (`[Storage] Improve error message in assertions`) and 069beddb1e (`[Storage] Fix for panics in state update code`) into the v1.41 release branch.
- Relaxes panicking assertions in `StateSummary::update` and `State::update` to `ensure`, handling rare fork scenarios where the persisted version advances past a block's parent version during speculative execution.
- Improves error messages in related storage assertions.

## Test plan
- [ ] Existing tests in `speculative_state_workflow` updated and passing
- [ ] CI passes on release branch

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core state update paths by converting previously panicking assertions into runtime errors; behavior changes under rare fork/version-skew scenarios could affect execution/commit flow if not handled correctly upstream.
> 
> **Overview**
> Prevents rare speculative-execution panics by turning version-ordering `assert!`s in `State::update` and `StateSummary::update` into logged, descriptive errors (`warn!` + `bail!`) when the persisted version is ahead (e.g., fork/version skew).
> 
> Propagates these new `Result` return types through the execution pipeline: `LedgerState::update_with_memorized_reads`/`State::update` now return `Result`, callers in `do_get_execution_output` handle failures with `?`, and the `speculative_state_workflow` test is updated to unwrap the new fallible API. Also removes the unused `aptos-rate-limiter` entry from `Cargo.lock`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 40b62ac997a12c3c997c2ba31e66255a6ec550d2. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Introduce Aggregated type for PVSS aggregation in aptos-dkg
items.body:
## Description
In upcoming modifications of `chunky`, its `Transcript` will store affine elements rather than projective elements in order to significantly improve and simplify the code in various places. The only downside of this change is that this would make naive usage of `aggregate_with()` rather slow, so this PR introduces an `Aggregated` type for this purpose. For simplicity, the aim of this PR is to _not_ change any of the existing logic, only to introduce a new trait and new methods.

Then in a future PR, two things need to happen:
- Chunky will get a proper `Aggregated` associated type with all the affine elements made projective again, since this allows for fast aggregation.
- The DKG code will be changed so it does _not_ normalize after each invocation of `aggregate_with()`, but should only do this after all aggregation is done.

## How Has This Been Tested?

Existing tests.

## Key Areas to Review

It is important that this PR does not change the logic of `das` transcript aggregation, as it claims.

## Type of Change
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Breaking change

items.title: [dkg] Add chunky DKG types and module structure
items.body:
## Summary

Adds the `dkg/src/chunky/` module structure with types and stubs.

### `types.rs` - Network message types
- `ChunkyDKGTranscriptRequest/Response` - for collecting transcripts from peers
- `MissingTranscriptRequest/Response` - for fetching missing transcripts
- `AggregatedSubtranscript` - aggregated result with contributors
- `CertifiedAggregatedSubtranscript` - aggregated result with multi-sig

### Module structure (stubs for subsequent PRs)
- `agg_subtrx_producer.rs` - aggregated subtranscript producer
- `subtrx_cert_producer.rs` - subtranscript certificate producer  
- `dkg_manager.rs` - DKG state machine
- `missing_transcript_fetcher.rs` - transcript fetcher

### Dependencies
- Adds `aptos-dkg`, `aptos-batch-encryption`, `aptos-short-hex-str`, `serde_bytes`

items.title: [framework] Add abort messages to some native functions
items.body:
## Description
<!-- Please include a summary of the change, including which issue it fixes or what feature it adds. Include relevant motivation, context and documentation as appropriate. List dependencies that are required for this change, if any. -->

Co-authored by Claude Code.

Improved runtime error messages in native functions by adding contextual information to abort conditions and extracting hard-coded abort codes to named constants.

**Note:** Abort messages will be discarded by the VM until the feature flag `VM_BINARY_FORMAT_V10` is enabled, so these changes will not affect existing behaviour until that flag is activated.


### Types of Changes

1. Added abort messages with contextual details where it makes sense.
2. Added abort messages for abort codes lacking descriptions in the Move module.
3. Extracted hard-coded abort codes to constants (mirroring the Move module).

### Changes by File

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `account.rs` | Added message for `NFE_UNABLE_TO_PARSE_ADDRESS` (no description in Move module) |
| `account_abstraction.rs` | Extracted `ENOT_MASTER_SIGNER` + added message (no description in Move module) |
| `aggregator_natives/aggregator_v2.rs` | Added type validation and string length messages with actual vs. maximum values |
| `cryptography/algebra/mod.rs` | Added message for `E_TOO_MUCH_MEMORY_USED` (no description in Move module) with current/new/limit memory values |
| `cryptography/bulletproofs.rs` | Added messages for `NFE_RANGE_NOT_SUPPORTED` (supported ranges), `NFE_BATCH_SIZE_NOT_SUPPORTED` (supported sizes), `NFE_VECTOR_LENGTHS_MISMATCH` (actual lengths) |
| `cryptography/ed25519.rs` | Added message for `E_WRONG_PUBKEY_SIZE` with expected vs. actual length |
| `cryptography/ristretto255_point.rs` | Added message for `E_TOO_MANY_POINTS_CREATED` with current count and limit |
| `cryptography/secp256k1.rs` | Added messages for `NFE_DESERIALIZE` (message/recovery ID/signature parsing with error

items.title: Rust 1.91
items.body:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Toolchain and compiler (Rust/Clang) bumps can surface new lints or behavioral differences across the workspace; code changes are mostly mechanical but touch many modules and test paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Upgrades the Rust toolchain to `1.91.1` and updates build/setup tooling to use Clang/LLVM `21` (Docker builder + `scripts/dev_setup.sh`).
> 
> Refactors numerous tests and helpers to pass transactions/events via slice/array literals (e.g. `&[txn]` / `std::slice::from_ref`) instead of allocating `Vec`s, and simplifies a simulation test assertion.
> 
> Replaces manual `impl Default` blocks with `#[derive(Default)]` + `#[default]` variants across multiple enums (CLI/config options, consensus/DKG state machines, execution mode, restore mode, verification scope, transaction auxiliary data), aligning with newer Rust idioms.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 5bcfc8c2833eaf57f5f0945a42084def5c579757. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: [dkg] Integrate ChunkyDKGManager with epoch manager
items.body: ## Summary Integrates `ChunkyDKGManager` with the epoch manager to run chunky DKG during reconfiguration. ### `epoch_manager.rs` - Adds `ChunkyDKGManager` lifecycle management (start/stop) - Routes chunky DKG messages (`ChunkyTranscriptRequest`, `SubtranscriptSignatureRequest`, etc.) to the manager - Handles `ChunkyDKGStartEvent` to trigger new DKG sessions - Manages separate RPC channels for regular DKG vs chunky DKG ### `state_sync.rs` - Subscribes to `0x1::chunky_dkg::ChunkyDKGStartEvent` in addition to regular DKG events ### `agg_trx_producer.rs` - Refactors to accept `Arc` for sharing with chunky DKG ### `api/types/transaction.rs` - Adds placeholder for `ChunkyDKGResult` API type conversion Cursor Bugbot reviewed your changes and found no issues for commit 92c0c23

items.title: [dkg] Add ChunkyDKGManager and missing transcript fetcher
items.body: ## Summary Implements the `ChunkyDKGManager` state machine and `MissingTranscriptFetcher`. ### `dkg_manager.rs` - Main coordinator State machine with transitions: - `Init` → `AwaitSubtranscriptAggregation` (on DKG start event) - `AwaitSubtranscriptAggregation` → `AwaitAggregatedSubtranscriptCertification` (when quorum aggregated) - `AwaitAggregatedSubtranscriptCertification` → `Finished` (when certified) Key methods: - `start_new_session` - generates transcript, starts aggregation - `on_aggregated_subtranscript` - starts certification phase - `on_certified_aggregated_subtranscript` - submits to validator txn pool - `process_dkg_rpc_request` - handles transcript requests from peers ### `missing_transcript_fetcher.rs` - Fetches transcripts from validators who haven't responded - Used to ensure transcript collection completes even with slow peers Cursor Bugbot found 2 potential issues for commit f4f5998

items.title: [telemetry] allow untrusted telemetry only on some paths
items.body:
## Description
<!-- Please include a summary of the change, including which issue it fixes or what feature it adds. Include relevant motivation, context and documentation as appropriate. List dependencies that are required for this change, if any. -->

Allow configs for a custom contract to support untrusted telemetry only. This is useful when building the Shelby telemetry config:
* Only allow trusted telemetry from SPs by checking onchain address list
* Only allow untrusted telemetry from RPCs (for now)

This also allows better filtering by `NodeType` on the backend

So now we can do something like this:

```
custom_contract_configs:
  # Open telemetry mode - no on_chain_auth, all nodes treated as unknown
  - name: "community_telemetry"
    allow_unknown_nodes: true  # REQUIRED when on_chain_auth is missing
    node_type_name: "CommunityNode"  # Labels outgoing metrics
    metrics_sinks:
      - endpoint_urls: {vm: "http://vm:8428/api/v1/import/prometheus"}
        auth_type: none
```

## How Has This Been Tested?
<!--
- Please ensure that the functionality introduced by this change is well tested and verified to work as expected.
- Ensure tests cover both happy and unhappy paths.
- List and link relevant tests.
-->

Tests pass

- Highlight any areas where you are particularly concerned or unsure about the code's impact on the change. This can include potential performance or security issues, or compatibility with existing features.
-->

- [ ] Bug fix

- [ ] Refactoring

items.title: Add BlockMetadataExt V2 with Decryption Key Support
items.body:
## Description
<!-- Please include a summary of the change, including which issue it fixes or what feature it adds. Include relevant motivation, context and documentation as appropriate. List dependencies that are required for this change, if any. -->

Adds `BlockMetadataExt::V2` variant that includes a decryption key alongside randomness, enabling encrypted mempool transactions to be decrypted per-block.

When enabled and wired up completely, it should produce additional field is block metadata like below.

```
...
"block_metadata_extension": {
      "randomness": null,
      "decryption_key": "0x309592dd04ae787f48b63f263f18084a80c3ea43c9e75318ac5600f6c7a68b02fb2fec5ae469dd9444a6cc966ff7500c98",
      "type": "v2"
},
"type": "block_metadata_transaction"
```

## Changes

- Added `BlockMetadataExt::V2` with optional `DecryptionKey` field
- Updated VM to execute `block_prologue_ext_v2` with decryption key
- Added new `decryption.move` module for per-block decryption key storage
- Updated consensus pipeline to construct V2 block metadata
- Added API support for V2 in transaction responses

items.title: Dependency security cleanup
items.body: ### Description Addressed several security vulnerabilities in project dependencies by introducing `pnpm.overrides` and updating the lockfile to use patched versions. This resolves high, moderate, and low severity issues originating from `glob` (via `sucrase`), `lodash`, `js-yaml`, and `diff`. ### Test Plan 1. Run `pnpm install` to ensure dependencies are installed correctly. 2. Run `pnpm audit` to confirm "No known vulnerabilities found". 3. Run `pnpm build` to verify the project builds successfully. 4. Run `pnpm lint` to ensure code quality checks pass. ### Related Links ### Checklist - [x] Have you ran `pnpm fmt`? - [x] Have you updated the `CHANGELOG.md`? ---

items.title: Bun HTTP/2 compatibility error
items.body: ### Description Implements a check in the `AptosConfig` constructor to detect if the SDK is running in a Bun environment. If detected, and HTTP/2 is not explicitly disabled (`clientConfig: { http2: false }`), an error is thrown to prevent compatibility issues, as Bun's HTTP/2 support is not fully mature. This proactively guides users to a known working configuration. ### Test Plan Unit tests have been added to `aptosConfig.test.ts` and `helpers.test.ts` covering: - `isBun()` function returning `true` or `false` based on the environment. - `AptosConfig` throwing an error when in Bun with HTTP/2 enabled (default or explicit `true`). - `AptosConfig` not throwing an error when in Bun with HTTP/2 explicitly disabled. - `AptosConfig` not throwing an error when not in a Bun environment. All tests pass by running `pnpm test`. ### Related Links ### Checklist - [x] Have you ran `pnpm fmt`? - [x] Have you updated the `CHANGELOG.md`? --- [Slack Thread](https://aptos-org.slack.com/archives/C05NLAKJM9U/p1770119192441329?thread_ts=1770119192.441329&cid=C05NLAKJM9U)

items.title: Documentation CI stability
items.body: Fix documentation CI failure by using nightly Rust for the `deploy-docs` job, as the `doc_cfg` feature requires it. ---

items.title: Fix e2e tests to actually run in CI
items.body:
## Summary

- **Fixed the core bug**: The 26 e2e tests are all marked `#[ignore]` but the CI workflow and local script were missing `-- --ignored`, so tests were compiled but silently skipped every run
- **Improved e2e workflow robustness**: localnet logs captured to file, printed on failure for debugging, explicit failure if localnet/faucet don't start in time
- **Fixed local script** (`scripts/run-e2e.sh`): same `-- --ignored` fix, test filtering still works

## Context

The `#[ignore]` attribute on e2e tests is intentional — it prevents them from accidentally running during `cargo test --all-features` in `ci.yml` (where no localnet is available). The `e2e` feature flag controls compilation, while `#[ignore]` controls runtime execution. The e2e workflow needs `-- --ignored` to opt in.

## Test plan

- [ ] E2E workflow should now actually execute all 26 ignored tests against localnet
- [ ] `ci.yml` with `--all-features` should still skip e2e tests (no localnet)
- [ ] `./scripts/run-e2e.sh` runs tests locally with `-- --ignored`
- [ ] `./scripts/run-e2e.sh account` still filters correctly

items.title: Fix Solana multi-signer transaction order for Phantom wallet
items.body:
Phantom wallet's Lighthouse security system was blocking CCTP transfer transactions on mainnet, flagging them as potentially malicious. This occurred because multi-signer transactions weren't following the correct signing order.

## Changes

Solana CCTP transactions from the Wormhole SDK require multiple signers. In addition to the user's wallet, the SDK generates ephemeral keypairs for protocol-specific accounts like message nonce accounts and token authority accounts. These aren't other users - they're keypairs the SDK creates internally to satisfy Solana's account model requirements for the CCTP program.

The SDK passes these additional signers via `request.transaction.signers`, which our code then needs to apply with `partialSign()`.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Small, localized change to Solana transaction signing order; main risk is unintended incompatibility with other wallets/transaction types if they expect the previous signer ordering.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes Solana multi-signer transaction signing order to satisfy Phantom Lighthouse security checks.
> 
> `signAndSendTransaction` now has the wallet sign the transaction first, then applies any extra Wormhole/CCTP `request.transaction.signers` via `partialSign()` afterward, and removes the earlier `partialSign()` done during priority-fee instruction setup. A changeset bumps `@aptos-labs/cross-chain-core` as a patch release.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e6817503da04bbddb57e41b4b4a1b46242fb5342. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

items.title: Support gas station for cctp withdraw transactions
items.body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches transaction signing/submission paths for Wormhole CCTP withdraws and introduces a new submission mechanism (gas station client), which can affect transaction validity and fee payer behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds **Aptos Gas Station** support for CCTP *withdraw* transactions by allowing `sponsorAccount` to be either a local `Account` or a per-network `GasStationApiKey` map, and wiring that through the Next.js demo components/config.
> 
> In `cross-chain-core`, the Wormhole Aptos signer now configures the ts-sdk with a `GasStationTransactionSubmitter` when a gas-station key is provided, while only applying `signAsFeePayer` when the sponsor is an actual `Account`; the package also adds `@aptos-labs/gas-station-client` and includes a changeset bump.
>

items.title: fix: improve cross-chain config and Solana RPC handling
items.body:
## Summary

This PR improves the cross-chain configuration and fixes Solana RPC handling issues.

## Changes

### Cross-chain Core
- Pass `crossChainCore` to `Signer` for proper RPC resolution
- Configure Wormhole SDK with custom Solana RPC
- Simplify `Signer` constructor by deriving `dappNetwork` from `crossChainCore`
- Fix network-specific RPC fallback logic in `SolanaSigner`

### Demo App (nextjs-x-chain)
- Consolidate configuration into shared `src/config/index.ts` module
- Remove duplicate `CrossChainCore` initializations
- Use environment variable (`NEXT_PUBLIC_SOLANA_RPC_MAINNET`) for Solana mainnet RPC
- Single source of truth for `DAPP_NETWORK`

## Testing
- Updated tests to reflect simplified `Signer` constructor

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches cross-chain transaction signing/submission and RPC endpoint selection, which can affect transfer/withdraw reliability across networks; changes are localized but impact runtime interactions with multiple chains.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves `@aptos-labs/cross-chain-core` RPC/network handling by making `Signer` require a `CrossChainCore` reference (so it can derive `aptosNetwork` and resolve Solana RPC), wiring that through Wormhole transfer/withdraw flows, and passing Solana RPC into the Wormhole SDK context.
> 
> Updates Aptos signers to respect the dapp’s configured network (instead of hardcoding testnet), adds network to `AptosLocalSigner`, and adjusts Solana RPC fallback to prefer dapp config then chain defaults. Also updates mainnet Solana `defaultRpc`.
> 
> Refactors the `nextjs-x-chain` demo to a shared `src/config/index.ts` singleton for `DAPP_NETWORK`, `crossChainCore/provider`, and signer accounts, removing duplicated initialization and simplifying `USDCBalanceProvider` usage; tests/docs/formatting are updated accordingly and a changeset bumps `cross-chain-core` minor.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 8986cbff558971e

items.title: fix(wallet-adapter-react): support async autoConnect and late wallet injection
items.body:
## Summary

Fixes multiple issues with `autoConnect` functionality that prevented automatic wallet reconnection in certain scenarios.

## Problems Fixed

### 1. Async `autoConnect` prop not working
If `autoConnect` started as `false` and later became `true` (e.g., after fetching user preferences from an API or localStorage hydration in SSR apps), auto-connect would never trigger.

### 2. Late wallet injection not working
If a wallet extension injected after the initial render (due to network latency, slow script loading, etc.), auto-connect would fail because the attempt was already marked as "done" before the wallet registered.

### 3. localStorage race condition in example apps
The `AutoConnectProvider` in the example apps had a race condition where `localStorage.removeItem` was called on initial mount before `localStorage.getItem` could read the stored value.

## Solution

Moved `didAttemptAutoConnectRef.current = true` to only be set when:
1. There's no stored wallet name (nothing to connect to)
2. We actually find the wallet and are about to connect

This allows the autoConnect effect to retry when:
- `autoConnect` prop changes from `false` to `true`
- New wallets register via the `standardWalletsAdded` event

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/wallet-adapter-react/src/WalletProvider.tsx` | Fix autoConnect logic to support async and late wallet scenarios |
| `packages/wallet-adapter-react/tests/WalletProvider.test.tsx` | Added 3 new tests for async autoConnect scenarios |
| `apps/nextjs-example/src/components/AutoConnectProvider.tsx` | Fix localStorage race condition |
| `apps/nextjs-x-chain/src/components/AutoConnectProvider.tsx` | Fix localStorage race condition |

## Testing

- ✅ 61 unit tests pass
- ✅ Manual testing with Petra wallet confirms auto-reconnect works

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the wallet auto-connect state machine and loading transitions, whic

items.title: add test for randomness and bump cli version to 7.14.2
Package size limit analysis
items.body: Revert dependency updates to fix Vercel 5MB serverless function limit caused by an unused Firestore package increase. The `firebase` package update from v12.7.0 to v12.8.0 pulled in `@firebase/firestore@4.10.0`, which introduced a new "Firestore Pipelines" feature, nearly doubling its size (+21.6MB). This project only uses `firebase/app` and `firebase/auth`, but the monolithic `firebase` package caused the overall bundle to exceed the 5MB limit. This PR reverts the problematic dependency updates and includes a detailed analysis document (`PACKAGE_SIZE_ANALYSIS.md`). ---

items.title: Cursor/documentation size limits 6cd3
items.body:
## Summary
 
This PR addresses Vercel's 5MB serverless function body limit by making the site fully static and adding build size analysis tooling.
 
## Problem
 
The site was hitting Vercel's 5MB limit due to:
1. **Dynamic OG image API** - Forced SSR mode, creating a 97MB `_render.func` bundle
2. **Large content collections** - All MDX content bundled into server chunks
 
## Solution
 
### 1. Remove OG Image API (SSR → Static)
- Deleted `src/pages/api/og-image.png.ts` (the only `prerender=false` route)
- Updated `Head.astro` to use static `/schema-logo.png` instead
- Site is now fully static - no serverless functions needed except middleware
 
### 2. Temporarily Disable Spanish Translations
- Moved `src/content/docs/es/` to `src/content/_docs_es_disabled/`
- Reduces content bundle size by ~3.5MB (344 files)
- Can be restored later: `mv src/content/_docs_es_disabled src/content/docs/es`
 
### 3. Add .md File Access via GitHub Redirect
- Users can access raw markdown by appending `.md` to any URL
- Example: `/build/cli.md` → redirects to GitHub raw content
- Middleware only handles redirect logic (~14KB), not file serving
 
### 4. Add Build Size Analysis
- Logs file sizes after build and before deployment
- Shows per-function sizes with warnings for files >4MB
 
## Trade-offs
 
- **OG Images**: Now use static logo instead of dynamic per-page images
- **Spanish Docs**: Temporarily unavailable (content preserved in repo)

items.title: Documentation size limits
items.body: This pull request contains changes generated by a Cursor Cloud Agent

items.title: revert: remove markdown file access feature
items.body:
## Summary

Reverts the markdown file access feature and all fix attempts due to Vercel's 5MB deployment limit.

### Reverted commits:
- `bd5d364` - fix: use Astro output dir instead of waiting for Vercel dir (#352)
- `98b4aee` - fix: replace dynamic .md route with build-time static generation (#351)
- `bc2bfba` - feat: add markdown file access by appending .md to page paths

### Why

All approaches hit Vercel's 5MB limit:
1. **Dynamic route with `getStaticPaths`**: 870 route entries → manifest too large
2. **Static file generation**: 870 files → deployment manifest too large  
3. **Bundled JSON content**: 6MB → function bundle too large

A different architecture is needed (e.g., external storage, edge functions with streaming, or GitHub redirects).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: fix: replace dynamic .md route with build-time static generation
items.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the "Body exceeded 5mb limit" error on Vercel when accessing `.md` file URLs
- The dynamic route in `[...slug].md.ts` was generating 870+ route entries, causing the Vercel route manifest to exceed 5MB
- Replaced with an Astro integration that copies `.mdx` files directly to `.vercel/output/static/` as `.md` files during build

## Changes

- **Created** `src/integrations/markdownFiles.ts` - Build-time static file generation
- **Updated** `astro.config.mjs` - Register the new integration
- **Updated** `vercel.json` - Add Content-Type and Cache-Control headers for `.md` files
- **Deleted** `src/pages/[...slug].md.ts` - Remove the problematic dynamic route

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `pnpm build` completes without errors
- [ ] Check `.vercel/output/static/*.md` files exist after build
- [ ] Deploy to Vercel preview and verify no 5MB error
- [ ] Test URLs: `/build/cli.md`, `/es/build/cli.md`, `/zh/build/cli.md`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: Markdown page access
items.body: Add a feature to serve documentation pages as raw markdown by appending `.md` to their URL. This provides direct access to the source content, bypassing standard page rendering and internationalization redirects. --- [Slack Thread](https://aptos-org.slack.com/archives/C03EG004E56/p1770261219248539?thread_ts=1770261219.248539&cid=C03EG004E56)

items.title: Documentation file and symlink
items.body: Adds `CLAUDE.md` with AI agent guidelines and a symlink `AGENTS.md`, and completes the Chinese glossary translation to fulfill documentation requirements. ---

items.title: Explorer local device prompts
items.body: ### Description Previously, the explorer would attempt to detect a local Aptos node on page load, often triggering unwanted browser prompts for local device connections. This PR modifies the local network detection mechanism to only activate when the user explicitly selects "localnet" from the network dropdown. The "localnet" option is now always visible in the network selector, allowing users to opt-in to local network connections without unsolicited prompts. ### Related Links ### Checklist ---
